project.name: tsnum
project.summary: Foundation TypeScript numerical computing library family whose public npm contract is @sylphx/numpy, with TypeScript, native, and WASM backend paths, benchmarks, tests, and package exports.
goals: Provide a typed, functional-first numerical computing API for JavaScript and TypeScript consumers.
Maintain the package family around the @sylphx/numpy public contract with NumPy-compatible spelling, array semantics, and explicit backend boundaries.
Keep TypeScript, native, and WASM backend behavior coherent through package exports, tests, and benchmarks.
Publish only documented package exports and performance claims that can be reproduced by repository-local evidence.
nonGoals: Own NumPy upstream behavior, scientific correctness beyond the documented package contract, or downstream application modeling decisions.
Own consumer product numerical policy, visualization, storage, or UI behavior.
Publish enterprise doctrine, project manifest schema, org rulesets, rollout issue reconciliation, or shared CI and release policy.

owns.name: sylphx-numpy-core-package
owns.description: The @sylphx/numpy package exports, TypeScript implementation, tests, and documented numerical API behavior.

owns.name: sylphx-numpy-wasm-backend
owns.description: Rust/WASM backend package and compatibility expectations for accelerated numerical operations.

owns.name: numpy-native-backend-path
owns.description: Rust/N-API native kernel package, native BLAS backend integration point, and compatibility expectations for Python parity hot paths.

owns.name: sylphx-numpy-benchmark-evidence
owns.description: Repository-local benchmark and performance documentation used to support package performance claims.

doesNotOwn: NumPy upstream behavior, consumer scientific modeling decisions, or downstream application numerical policy.
Visualization, persistence, UI, or product-specific workflows built on top of numerical arrays.
Enterprise doctrine, project manifest schema, rollout issue reconciliation, org rulesets, or shared workflow implementation.

allowedDependencies.surface: project manifest schema, standards, and rollout audit

forbiddenCouplings: Do not require consumers to import internal source paths instead of documented package exports.
Do not encode one consumer product's numerical policy or dataset assumptions as hidden library behavior.
Do not publish performance claims that lack reproducible benchmark evidence in this repository.
Do not publish enterprise process policy from this repository instead of SylphxAI/doctrine.

delivery.deployPath: Repo-local GitHub Actions CI validates pull requests, merge queue candidates, and main pushes with Bun install, build, tests, the enforced Python parity benchmark, and generated report freshness. Package publication is manually triggered through .github/workflows/release.yml, delegates to the central Sylphx release workflow, and runs release:preflight before publish plus release:readback after publish.
delivery.productionProof: Merged package changes require green CI on the PR and main push, including the uploaded enforced Python parity report artifact. Published package changes additionally require benchmark evidence for performance-sensitive claims, all-public-package registry readback after release, and consumer smoke proof for affected exports.

packageRelease.releaseIntent: Changesets under .changeset/ are the release-intent source. Direct root release is disabled; package publication must go through the manual Release workflow.
packageRelease.versionPr: Declared through the central Sylphx reusable release workflow, which runs changesets/action to create GitHub Actions bot-authored version PRs when release intent exists. A dedicated Sylphx release-bot/GitHub App identity remains a release-hardening gap before commercial-grade publish.
packageRelease.publisher: Declared through the protected central Sylphx reusable release workflow with id-token: write OIDC posture and inherited bounded NPM_TOKEN fallback.
packageRelease.publishProof: Required before publish: release:preflight must pass build, tests, and bench:python-parity:enforce. Required after publish: release:readback must verify every public workspace npm package version. Provenance/attestation evidence, changelog evidence, and consumer smoke proof are still required for affected exports.

gaps.description: Repo-local CI validates pull requests, merge queue candidates, and main pushes, but full doctrine adoption still needs stable fan-in admission contexts, Sylphx runner profile policy, and workflow sanity checks.

Manual release workflow, preflight, and npm readback are declared, but no successful @sylphx/numpy publish/readback has been observed yet. Current Python speed parity gaps intentionally block release:preflight.
The central reusable workflow runs Changesets automation, but a dedicated Sylphx release-bot/GitHub App version-PR identity is not proven from this repo-local evidence.

Latest main CI evidence passes checksum parity and 6 of 7 covered speed rows on macOS arm64 native BLAS, but matmul_128 remains above the 1.05x Python speed target and release preflight remains intentionally blocked until covered-operation speed parity passes.
gaps.target: Before package publication or full Python-speed marketing
